findings.title: Project funds can be permanently locked because refund progress is compared against NFT count instead of bid-record count

best_agent_match.title: Project funds can be permanently locked by comparing bidder progress to NFT count

findings.title: Any bidder can permanently block refund processing by reverting on ETH receipt

best_agent_match.title: Any bidder can brick refund processing by reverting on ETH receipt

findings.title: Emergency withdrawal can refund bidders in full even after they already received NFTs

best_agent_match.title: Emergency withdrawal can give bidders a full refund even after they already received NFTs

findings.title: Zero-amount bids create dummy records that can satisfy the withdrawal gate before all real refunds are processed

best_agent_match.title: Zero-amount bids let sybils create dummy bidder records and unlock premature project withdrawal

rejected_candidates.title: Reentrancy vulnerability in _bid() function
rejected_candidates.reason: The function writes the bidder record, `totalBids`, and `totalBidValue` before the refund call. Reentry observes updated state and does not bypass bid limits or create a double-refund path.

rejected_candidates.title: Reentrancy vulnerability in processRefunds() function
rejected_candidates.reason: `allBids[i].finalProcess` is set before the external call, so a reentrant invocation cannot reclaim the same refund. Reentry may recurse into already-marked records but does not create duplicate payouts.

rejected_candidates.title: Reentrancy vulnerability in emergencyWithdraw() function
rejected_candidates.reason: `finalProcess` is set to `2` before sending ETH, so a reentrant call fails the `finalProcess == 0` check and cannot withdraw multiple times.

rejected_candidates.title: Integer underflow in processRefunds() refund calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a descending-price auction, and `getPrice()` is clamped to `expiresAt`, so the final settlement price cannot exceed a bidder's recorded price. `bidData.price - price` therefore does not underflow in normal execution.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner can bypass airdrop completion check
rejected_candidates.reason: This requires a malicious or compromised owner abusing the explicit `onlyOwner` power to set the NFT contract address. It is a privileged-admin trust issue rather than a permissionless protocol flaw.

rejected_candidates.title: No deadline extension for last-minute bidders
rejected_candidates.reason: This is auction design/fairness, not a security vulnerability causing fund loss, insolvency, lockup, or DoS.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential griefing via front-running bids
rejected_candidates.reason: This describes ordinary mempool competition without a concrete invariant break or exploit path beyond normal auction ordering risk.
